item.title: clawhq demo --cloud — zero-config cloud demo (phase 2)
item.intent: clawhq demo --cloud gives any user a zero-Docker path to experiencing a live agent in 60 seconds. Just a DO API token. Ephemeral droplet self-destructs on exit — no orphaned instances, no ongoing cost. This is the conversion tool for the sovereignty pitch.
item.description: Phase 2 of FEAT-002 demo command. After CQ-044 (DO adapter) ships, add --cloud flag to 'clawhq demo' that provisions an ephemeral DigitalOcean droplet, spins up the demo agent in the cloud, opens the browser chat UI pointing at the cloud URL, and destroys the droplet on exit. Zero Docker requirement on the user's machine — they just need a DO API token. Scope: add --cloud flag to existing demo command; provision ephemeral DO droplet using CQ-043 engine + CQ-044 adapter; inject demo blueprint + mock LLM config via cloud-init (CQ-048); open browser pointing at cloud agent URL; cleanup droplet on SIGINT/exit. Cost: minimal — cx1 droplet for ~60s demo = fractions of a cent.
steps: Add --cloud flag to existing clawhq demo command in src/cli/index.ts
Check for DIGITALOCEAN_TOKEN env var; prompt user if not set
Provision ephemeral DO droplet via provisioning engine (smallest size, nearest region)
Inject demo blueprint + mock LLM config via cloud-init template (CQ-048)
Poll until agent responds healthy (max 3 min timeout with progress spinner)
Open browser to cloud agent chat URL
Register SIGINT/exit handler to destroy droplet immediately on exit
Show estimated cost (fractions of a cent) before provisioning and confirm
acceptanceCriteria: clawhq demo --cloud provisions a cloud agent without Docker on the users machine
Only a DIGITALOCEAN_TOKEN is required — no other setup
Cost estimate shown before provisioning; user must confirm
Browser opens automatically to live cloud chat UI within 3 minutes
SIGINT destroys the droplet immediately — no orphaned instances
Exit path destroys droplet even on crash (best-effort cleanup with user warning)
